reasons: Tigreal can group enemies, giving Kagura a clearer umbrella combo target area.
Kagura can follow the engage with burst, pull, and reposition tools.

proof.summary: Tigreal creates the grouped engage, while Kagura follows with umbrella burst and control.
worksBestWhen: Kagura has umbrella position ready before Tigreal commits.
Kagura is close enough to follow the grouped control.
failureCases: Tigreal engages outside Kagura's combo range.
Enemies spread before Kagura's delayed control lands.

reasons: Atlas can drag enemies together, making Kagura's delayed pull and burst easier to land.
Kagura can punish the grouped enemies from a safer mid-range position.

proof.summary: Atlas groups targets, then Kagura layers umbrella combo damage onto the grouped area.
worksBestWhen: Kagura saves her main combo until after Atlas confirms the drag.
Atlas drags enemies into Kagura's umbrella range.
failureCases: Enemies spread before Kagura's delayed control lands.

reasons: Chou can isolate or kick a priority target into Kagura's damage range.
Kagura can follow the displaced target with umbrella burst and control.

proof.summary: Chou starts a single-target displacement, and Kagura follows with burst before the target stabilizes.
worksBestWhen: Chou kicks the target toward Kagura or into a predictable path.
Kagura tracks the kick direction before committing her combo.
failureCases: Chou kicks the target away from Kagura's umbrella position.
Kagura uses her combo before the displacement is confirmed.

reasons: Franco can pull one enemy into Kagura's combo range.
Kagura can add burst and crowd control after the pull, turning the catch into a kill threat.

proof.summary: Franco catches one target, while Kagura layers umbrella burst and control onto the displaced enemy.
worksBestWhen: Kagura has umbrella position ready before the pull lands.
failureCases: Franco catches a durable frontline target.

reasons: Kaja can suppress and drag a target into Kagura's prepared umbrella zone.
Kagura can follow the controlled target with burst and delayed pull before the target can freely reposition.

proof.summary: Kaja creates a controlled single-target setup, while Kagura uses umbrella burst and pull to finish the pickoff.
worksBestWhen: Kaja drags the target toward Kagura's umbrella position.
Kagura waits for Kaja's confirmed catch before using the main combo.
failureCases: Kaja catches a tank or drags the target outside Kagura's umbrella range.
Kagura uses her combo before Kaja confirms the target.
